config.description: AFPS schema wrapper for agent configuration (set once, reused across runs).

schema.description: Pure JSON Schema 2020-12 object

input.description: AFPS schema wrapper for per-run input.

schema.description: Pure JSON Schema 2020-12 object

output.description: AFPS schema wrapper for per-run output.

schema.description: Pure JSON Schema 2020-12 object

tools.description: Niveau 2 tool allowlist (optional). Either an array of selected tool names or the AFPS §4.4 wildcard literal '*' opting the agent into every upstream tool (requires integration's `allow_undeclared_tools: true`).

last_run.description: Summary of the most recent run (null if never run)

version_count.description: Number of published versions (0 for built-in agents)

has_unarchived_changes.description: Whether the active version has changes not yet archived as a version

yanked.description: Whether this version has been yanked

content.description: Primary content file extracted from the version ZIP

source_code.description: Secondary source file content (e.g. .ts), when present

yanked.description: Whether this version has been yanked

packageId.description: Source agent ID. NULL when the source agent has been deleted — the run row survives via `runs.package_id ON DELETE SET NULL` (migration 0017). Read `agent_scope` / `agent_name` for display in that case; re-running is not possible.

userId.description: Dashboard user ID that triggered the run (null for end-user/schedule runs)

result.description: What the run produced — the stable API contract for the run's deliverable, set when the run reaches a terminal status. `null` while the run is in flight, and on terminal runs that emitted neither structured output nor a report. Persisted even on failed runs (a run that reported and then failed keeps its partial deliverable).

output.description: Structured JSON emitted via the agent's `output` runtime tool. Validated against the agent's declared output schema when one exists — a schema mismatch flips the run to `failed` (with the validation errors in `error`) but the payload is still stored, never dropped.

text.description: Markdown report emitted via the agent's `report` runtime tool. Multiple report calls are concatenated in call order, joined with newlines. Capped at 256 KiB of UTF-8 — see `text_truncated`. The full untruncated report remains available as individual run-log entries (type='result', event='report').

text_truncated.description: Present and `true` when `text` exceeded the 256 KiB cap and was truncated at a UTF-8 character boundary. Absent otherwise.

token_usage.description: Snapshot of token consumption for the run. Snake-case keys match the AFPS wire format emitted by every runner (PiRunner / remote CLI / GitHub Action) and stored verbatim in JSONB.

version_label.description: Version label at run time (e.g. '1.0.0'). For draft runs this is the latest published version the draft sits on top of — read `version_ref` to know which definition actually executed.

version_ref.description: Unambiguous reference to the agent definition the run executed: 'draft' when the mutable draft ran with unpublished changes (or the agent has no published version), or the concrete semver (e.g. '2.1.0') when the run executed that published definition (or a draft identical to it).

proxy_label.description: Proxy label used at run time

model_label.description: Model label used at run time

model_source.description: Model source: 'system' (platform-provided) or 'org' (user-configured). Resolved at run creation — an org-default change between triggers applies to subsequent runs unless the run was pinned via the runAgent `modelId` override.

endUserId.description: End-user ID (eu_ prefix) if executed on behalf of an end-user

apiKeyId.description: API key ID that triggered the run (null for dashboard/schedule runs)

applicationId.description: Application ID (app_ prefix) that owns this run

metadata.description: Additional module-supplied metadata (e.g. usage-metering fields written by an optional module). Free-form; core does not define billing-specific keys.

config.description: Snapshot of the effective agent config (merged overrides) at run creation

config_override.description: Per-run config delta — the raw object the caller sent in the request body. `config` is the resolved (deep-merged) snapshot; `config_override` is the raw delta that the dashboard uses to badge 'default vs override'. Null when the run used persisted defaults verbatim.

user_name.description: Display name of the dashboard user who triggered the run (from profiles table)

end_user_name.description: Display name of the end-user (name or externalId fallback)

api_key_name.description: Name of the API key that triggered the run

schedule_name.description: Name of the schedule that triggered the run

runner_name.description: Human-friendly label for the runner that triggered the run — CLI host (`os.hostname()`), GitHub Action workflow, or whatever the caller passes via `X-Appstrate-Runner-Name`. Stamped at INSERT and never updated.

runner_kind.description: Free-form classifier driving the dashboard icon (`cli`, `github-action`, …). Sourced from `X-Appstrate-Runner-Kind` or inferred from the auth context.

agent_scope.description: Denormalized agent scope at run creation, including the leading `@` (e.g. `@myorg`). Survives rename, delete, or shadow compaction — the global run view falls back to this when the source package is gone.

agent_name.description: Denormalized agent name at run creation (see agent_scope).

package_ephemeral.description: Present on enriched run responses. True when the source package is an inline-run shadow (POST /api/runs/inline).

inline_manifest.description: Inline runs only. Snapshot of the manifest submitted at run time. Null once the shadow has been compacted (see INLINE_RUN_LIMITS.retention_days).

inline_prompt.description: Inline runs only. Snapshot of the prompt submitted at run time. Null once the shadow has been compacted.

unread.description: True when the requesting recipient has an unread notification for this run (issue #667). Per-recipient: derived from the notifications table for the current actor, so a dashboard user and an end-user see independent state. Drives the unread dot on run rows and the per-schedule unread count.

runNumber.description: Per-(app, package) monotonic counter assigned at run creation. Stable identifier for UI display.

runOrigin.description: Which runner drives this run: 'platform' (server-managed Docker container) or 'remote' (caller's host via signed events).

contextSnapshot.description: Runner-provided execution environment metadata (os, cli version, git sha, ...) stamped at run creation.

modelCredentialId.description: ID of the model_provider_credentials row resolved at run creation (audit + cost-attribution).

connection_overrides.description: Per-integration connection picks for this run (flat-connections mechanism #2). Flat map: `{ "@scope/integration": "<connection_id>" }` — one connection per integration; the chosen connection carries its own authKey. Loses to admin pins (#1).

dependency_overrides.description: Per-run dependency version overrides (#666). Flat map: `{ "@scope/skill": "draft" | "<semver|dist-tag>" }`. A `"draft"` value means the run consumed a dependency's mutable working copy — so it is NOT reproducible from `version_ref` alone. Null when the run resolved the manifest pins verbatim against published versions.

connections_used.description: Connections resolved for this run, projected from the internal snapshot for display. Null when the agent declares no integrations.

level.description: Log severity level. Non-admin users only receive info, warn, and error logs.

userId.description: Member actor the schedule runs as

endUserId.description: End-user actor the schedule runs as

applicationId.description: Application ID (app_ prefix) that owns this schedule

connection_overrides.description: Per-integration connection picks frozen on the schedule row (flat-connections mechanism #3). Flat map: `{ "@scope/integration": "<connection_id>" }`. Replayed on every fire; loses to admin pins (#1), beats actor-fallback (#4).

dependency_overrides.description: Per-dependency version overrides frozen on the schedule row (#666/#686). Flat map: `{ "@scope/dep": "draft" | "<semver|dist-tag>" }`; keys may name a declared skill OR integration. Forwarded to each fired run's `dependency_overrides` so a scheduled run resolves its dependencies exactly as the schedule froze them.

actor_name.description: Display name of the schedule actor

keyPrefix.description: First 8 chars of the key for identification

scopes.description: Permission scopes granted to this API key.

orgId.description: Owning organization ID (null for system packages)
Owning organization ID (null for system packages)

source_code.description: Secondary source file content (e.g. .ts for tools)

manifest_name.description: Manifest name (@scope/name) — may differ from package ID

has_unarchived_changes.description: Whether the active version has changes not yet archived as a version

apiShape.description: Protocol family. `null` for a built-in credential whose every model is managed (#727) — the binding is not exposed, so the endpoint doesn't reveal the provider.

baseUrl.description: Endpoint base URL. `null` for a managed-only built-in credential (see apiShape).

providerId.description: Canonical providerId backing the credential. Set when `authMode === 'oauth2'`.

available_model_ids.description: Model ids this credential is authorized to seed, persisted by model discovery (POST /:id/refresh-models, also fired after OAuth import) — the server-side authorization record gating model seeding. For `probe`-validation (API-key) providers these are empirically verified against the live credential; for `offline`-validation providers (subscription: codex, claude-code) these are the provider's static candidate set (∩ catalog), persisted with zero upstream calls. Null = discovery never ran. Per-credential because availability depends on the account's plan.

apiShape.description: Protocol family. `null` for managed models (`aliased: true`) — binding not exposed.

providerId.description: The credential's provider id (e.g. `anthropic`, `claude-code`, `codex`). Distinguishes subscription providers that share an `apiShape` with an API-key provider so clients route them to the right proxy path. `null` for managed models — binding not exposed.

providerName.description: The provider's human display name resolved from the model-provider registry by `providerId` (e.g. `OpenCode Go`, `OpenAI`). The authoritative label for grouping/badging a model by provider — `apiShape` is ambiguous (OpenCode Go and OpenAI both use `openai-completions`), so do NOT derive a provider label from it. `null` for managed models (binding not exposed) and for rows whose `providerId` has no registry entry.

baseUrl.description: Provider endpoint. `null` for managed models — binding not exposed.

modelId.description: Upstream model id. `null` for managed models — not exposed.

aliased.description: Managed-model flag. When true, the binding (`modelId`, `apiShape`, `baseUrl`, `credentialId`, capabilities/cost) is not exposed in this projection — these fields are `null`; render a managed badge.

iconUrl.description: Display-icon key for the UI (a client provider-icon key, e.g. `anthropic`, `openai`). A deliberate public choice on the model — decoupled from the provider, so a managed model can show an icon without exposing its binding. `null` means resolve the icon from the (visible) `apiShape`/`baseUrl`, or fall back to a generic icon.

credentialId.description: ID of the `model_provider_credentials` row. `null` for managed models — binding not exposed.

status.description: Upstream HTTP status when the provider answered at all — distinguishes 429 (retry later) from 404 (model not served).

OAuthTokenResponse.description: Resolved access token returned by `GET /internal/oauth-token/{id}` and `POST .../refresh`. Carries only the fields that change per refresh — provider invariants (baseUrl, …) live in the sidecar's boot-time `LlmProxyOauthConfig`. Wire-equivalent to the `OAuthTokenResponse` TS interface in `@appstrate/core/sidecar-types`.

expiresAt.description: Epoch milliseconds. null when expiry is unknown.

accountId.description: Abstract account/tenant identifier surfaced by the provider's `extractTokenIdentity` hook. The sidecar's identity layer (keyed by providerId from the boot config) decides which routing header to echo it as.

IntegrationCredentialsResponse.description: Live credentials + per-auth HTTP delivery plans + per-auth expiries for an installed integration. Returned by both `GET /internal/integration-credentials/{scope}/{name}` and `POST .../refresh` (identical shape). Feeds the sidecar's MITM `MitmCredentialSource.current()` + `.deliveryPlans()`. All wire keys are snake_case per AFPS (see `docs/CASING_CONVENTIONS.md` — internal sidecar↔platform endpoints share the Zone 1 default).

resource.description: RFC 8707 resource indicator declared by the manifest (`auths.{key}.resource`). AFPS §7.3 name — matches the RFC.

IntegrationAgentResolution.description: Per-integration connection verdict for an agent: which connection the next run uses (admin pin → run/schedule override → member pin → fallback + scope check), the annotated candidate list, and admin/member pin + blocked state. Computed by the same resolver the runtime uses.

AgentConnectionReadiness.description: Bulk integration connection readiness for an agent. `blocks_run`/`errors` mirror the run-kickoff 412 (run semantics); `integrations[]` carries every declared integration's management verdict for the Connexions tab.

blocks_run.description: True iff POST /api/agents/{scope}/{name}/run would reject with 412.

errors.description: Integration portion of the 412 envelope (same `field: integrations.<id>` shape as ProblemDetail.errors). Shares the single ResolutionFieldError component so the shape can't drift from the 412 error items.

run_blocking.description: True iff this integration is one of the run-blocking `errors`.

isDefault.description: Whether this is the default application

allowedRedirectDomains.description: Domains allowed for OAuth redirect callbacks

created_by.description: ID of the user who created the application

AgentManifest.description: AFPS Agent manifest extended with Appstrate platform fields. Standard fields are defined by the AFPS Agent schema. Most extension fields use the x- prefix per AFPS §10, with the exception of the Appstrate-specific top-level `runtime_tools` field documented below.

runtime_tools.description: Appstrate top-level extension: runtime tools the agent may use. Optional.

FileConstraintsMap.description: Upload constraints for file fields, keyed by property name. Lives at the AFPS wrapper level (outside the JSON Schema).

UIHintsMap.description: UI rendering hints for schema fields, keyed by property name. Lives at the AFPS wrapper level (outside the JSON Schema).

placeholder.description: Hint text shown before the user provides a value

LibraryPackageList.description: Packages of a single type visible to the org. Each entry carries an `installed_in` array listing the caller-org applications where the package is currently installed (empty array = not installed in any of the caller's apps).

source.description: Package origin (`local` for org-owned packages, `system` for built-in system packages).

name.description: Display name from the package draft manifest (`manifest.display_name`); falls back to the package id.

description.description: Description from the package draft manifest; empty string when not provided.

installed_in.description: Application ids (`app_…`) belonging to the caller's org where this package is installed.

ValidationError.description: Validation error. Body-level failures emit `code: "validation_failed"` with a populated `errors[]` array listing every offending field in one response. Single-field failures outside the body (query params, headers) still use `code: "invalid_request"` with the `param` pointer.
